tasks.title: Retrieve relevant information on Booking.com no-show disputes
Search for policies and similar cases regarding no-show and cancellations on Booking.com
Analyze retrieved data to identify resolution steps for the complaint
Formulate a complaint triage plan including communication and escalation procedures
Deliver a compact, actionable plan for handling the Booking.com no-show complaint

rag_output.summary:
- Support must verify the timeline when a property marks a reservation as no-show after the guest claims cancellation; any timeline conflicts should be escalated [1].  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show, support should consider the charge potentially refundable and gather cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner details before responding [2].  
- Support should determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or property); if the property charged directly, collect payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before proceeding [3].  

Sources: [1] refunds_policy.md, [2] refunds_policy.md, [3] payment_disputes.md

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: When the property marked the reservation as a no-show after the guest claims to have cancelled, support must verify the timeline before declining the request. Timeline conflicts should be escalated.

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the guest cancelled inside the free-cancellation window and the booking was not later marked as a no-show, support should treat the charge as potentially refundable and gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner before responding.

evidence.title: payment_disputes.md
evidence.excerpt: The first question is who collected the money: Booking.com or the property. If the property charged the guest directly, support should collect the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before deciding on next steps.

web_output.summary:
Task: How to report guest no-shows and request removal of guest reviews on Booking.com

Public evidence:
- To report guest no-shows, property partners must follow Booking.com's specific procedures outlined in their help section, which includes documenting the no-show and submitting the report through the partner portal [1].
- Booking.com provides guidelines for partners on how to request the removal of guest reviews, typically requiring evidence that the review violates content guidelines or is fraudulent [2].
- Both processes are managed through the Booking.com partner interface, ensuring proper handling and compliance with Booking.com's policies [1][2].

Sources: [1] https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/reservations/overbookings-no-shows/reporting-guest-no-shows-your-property, [2] https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/guest-reviews/general/how-request-removal-guest-review

evidence.title: Reporting guest no-shows at your property - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: You can mark the booking as a no-show. This prevents guests from automatically receiving a satisfaction survey when they didn't stay with you.

evidence.title: How to request the removal of a guest review - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: You canceled the reservation at least 24 hours before the scheduled check-in. The reservation is marked as a no-show and the review doesn't ...

evidence.title: Customer terms of service
evidence.excerpt: 2. If you cancel a booking or don't show up, any cancellation/no-show fee and any refund will depend on the Service Provider's cancellation/ ...

action_plan:
- Verify the timeline of the guest’s cancellation claim against the property’s no-show marking; escalate if there are discrepancies or conflicts in timing [internal 1].  
- Determine if the cancellation occurred within the free-cancellation window and identify who collected the payment (Booking.com or property); gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, payment ownership, and any relevant receipts or message trails before proceeding [internal 2,3].  
- Advise the property to report the no-show through the Booking.com partner portal with proper documentation, and if the guest left a review, guide the property on submitting a removal request with evidence if the review violates guidelines [public 1,2].
next_steps:
1. Request and review the guest’s cancellation timestamp and compare it with the property’s no-show marking time to identify any timeline discrepancies; escalate internally if conflicts arise.  
2. Confirm whether the cancellation was made within the free-cancellation window and determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or property); gather all relevant payment and policy documentation.  
3. If the property charged directly, collect payment receipts and message trails related to the cancellation and no-show marking for verification.  
4. Advise the property to report the no-show through the Booking.com partner portal with proper documentation and, if applicable, guide them on submitting a guest review removal request with supporting evidence.
final_answer:
Issue:  
A property marked a booking as a no-show after the guest claims they cancelled the reservation. There is a need to verify the accuracy of the no-show marking and determine the appropriate refund or dispute handling.

Internal policy:  
- Support must verify the timeline of the guest’s cancellation claim against the property’s no-show marking; any timeline conflicts should be escalated [1].  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show at that time, the charge may be refundable. Support should gather the cancellation timestamp, property cancellation policy, and payment ownership details before responding [2].  
- It is essential to determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or the property). If the property charged directly, support must collect payment receipts, message trails, and cancellation timelines before proceeding [3].

Public policy:  
- Properties must report guest no-shows by following Booking.com’s procedures via the partner portal, including proper documentation of the no-show [1].  
- To request removal of guest reviews, properties must submit evidence that the review violates Booking.com’s content guidelines or is fraudulent, also through the partner interface [2].  
- Both no-show reporting and review removal requests are managed exclusively through the Booking.com partner platform to ensure compliance [1][2].

Recommended action:  
- Verify and compare the guest’s cancellation timestamp with the property’s no-show marking time; escalate internally if there are discrepancies.  
- Confirm if the cancellation was within the free-cancellation window and identify who collected the payment. Collect all relevant documentation such as cancellation timestamps, property policies, payment receipts, and message trails.  
- Advise the property to report the no-show through the Booking.com partner portal with appropriate evidence. If the guest left a review, guide the property on how to submit a removal request with supporting proof if the review breaches guidelines.

Next steps:  
1. Request the guest’s cancellation timestamp and compare it with the property’s no-show marking time; escalate if timeline conflicts are found.  
2. Determine if the cancellation was within the free-cancellation period and identify payment ownership; gather all relevant documentation.  
3. If the property charged directly, obtain payment receipts and message history related to the cancellation and no-show.  
4. Instruct the property to report the no-show via the Booking.com partner portal with proper documentation. If applicable, assist the property in submitting a guest review removal request with evidence through the partner interface.

Cited Sources:
- https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/reservations/overbookings-no-shows/reporting-guest-no-shows-your-property
- https://www.booking.com/content/terms.html
- /Users/ganesamaniankolappan/Documents/Temp_work/Agent/data/rag/refunds_policy.md
- https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/guest-reviews/general/how-request-removal-guest-review
- /Users/ganesamaniankolappan/Documents/Temp_work/Agent/data/rag/payment_disputes.md
